name: Ascend Scheduler
description: Scheduler module for the Ascend CLI that handles cron expression parsing, next-run computation, and macOS launchd plist generation. Converts human-friendly schedule specifications to cron expressions, computes next scheduled run times, generates launchd configuration files, and manages loading/unloading of scheduled tasks on macOS.

types.name: CronFields
types.description: Dictionary representing parsed cron expression fields

functions.name: schedule_to_cron
functions.description: Convert human-friendly schedule specification to a standard 5-field cron expression (minute hour day-of-month month day-of-week)

inputs.name: weekdays
inputs.description: Run on weekdays (Mon-Fri) at specified time

inputs.name: weekly
inputs.description: Run weekly on specified day name (e.g., 'monday', 'tue')

inputs.name: biweekly
inputs.description: Run biweekly on specified day name (2nd and 4th occurrence)

inputs.name: monthly
inputs.description: Run monthly on specified comma-separated day numbers

inputs.name: quarterly
inputs.description: Run quarterly on first day of quarters (Jan, Apr, Jul, Oct)

error_cases.name: invalid_day
error_cases.condition: weekly or biweekly day name is not in _DAY_MAP

error_cases.name: no_frequency
error_cases.condition: No schedule frequency flag is set to True or provided

preconditions: At least one frequency flag must be set (daily, weekdays, weekly, biweekly, monthly, or quarterly)
If weekly or biweekly is set, the day name must be valid (sunday/sun through saturday/sat, case-insensitive)
postconditions: Returns a valid 5-field cron expression string

functions.name: parse_cron
functions.description: Parse a 5-field cron expression into its component parts

error_cases.name: invalid_field_count
error_cases.condition: expr does not contain exactly 5 fields after splitting on whitespace

preconditions: expr must contain exactly 5 whitespace-separated fields
postconditions: Returns dict with keys: minute, hour, dom, month, dow
Each value is a string that may contain *, ranges, or comma-separated lists

functions.name: _expand_field
functions.description: Expand a cron field specification into a set of integer values, handling wildcards, ranges, and comma-separated lists

inputs.name: field
inputs.description: Cron field string (may be *, a number, a range like 1-5, or comma-separated)

inputs.name: min_val
inputs.description: Minimum valid value for this field

inputs.name: max_val
inputs.description: Maximum valid value for this field

error_cases.name: invalid_integer
error_cases.condition: Field contains non-integer values that cannot be converted

preconditions: field must be either '*', an integer, a range (N-M), or comma-separated values
postconditions: Returns set of integers within [min_val, max_val]
If field is '*', returns all values from min_val to max_val inclusive

functions.name: compute_next_run
functions.description: Compute the next run time for a cron expression after a given datetime, implementing cron OR semantics for day-of-month and day-of-week

inputs.name: after
inputs.description: Reference datetime to compute next run after (defaults to now)

error_cases.name: invalid_cron
error_cases.condition: cron_expr cannot be parsed by parse_cron

preconditions: cron_expr must be a valid 5-field cron expression
postconditions: Returns ISO format datetime string '%Y-%m-%d %H:%M'
Returned datetime is strictly after 'after' parameter
Returned datetime matches cron expression constraints
Returns empty string if no match found within 366 days
side_effects: Calls datetime.now() if after is None

structured_side_effects.description: Reads current system time if after parameter is None

functions.name: describe_cron
functions.description: Convert a cron expression to a human-readable description string

error_cases.name: invalid_cron
error_cases.condition: cron_expr cannot be parsed by parse_cron

error_cases.name: invalid_field_value
error_cases.condition: _expand_field encounters invalid integer values when expanding dow

preconditions: cron_expr must be a valid 5-field cron expression
postconditions: Returns human-readable description of the schedule
Falls back to returning original cron_expr if pattern not recognized

functions.name: _find_ascend_path
functions.description: Locate the ascend executable path by checking system PATH and common installation locations
postconditions: Returns path to ascend executable if found
Returns 'ascend' as fallback if not found in any location
side_effects: Checks filesystem for existence of /usr/local/bin/ascend and ~/.local/bin/ascend

structured_side_effects.target: system PATH and common installation directories
structured_side_effects.description: Searches for ascend executable in PATH and checks specific directories

functions.name: _cron_to_calendar_intervals
functions.description: Convert a cron expression to launchd CalendarInterval XML format

error_cases.name: invalid_cron
error_cases.condition: cron_expr cannot be parsed by parse_cron

preconditions: cron_expr must be a valid 5-field cron expression
postconditions: Returns XML string representing launchd CalendarInterval
XML is either a single <dict> or <array> of <dict> elements
Each dict contains Hour, Minute, and optionally Weekday, Day, Month keys

functions.name: generate_plist
functions.description: Generate launchd plist metadata (label, path, log path) for a schedule. NOTE: Implementation appears incomplete - returns tuple instead of full plist content

inputs.name: schedules_dir
inputs.description: Directory where schedule logs will be stored

postconditions: Returns tuple of (label, ascend_path, log_path) - NOTE: type annotation says str but actually returns tuple

functions.name: write_plist
functions.description: Generate and write a launchd plist file for a schedule to ~/Library/LaunchAgents/

inputs.name: schedules_dir
inputs.description: Directory where schedule logs will be stored

error_cases.name: filesystem_error
error_cases.condition: Cannot create directory or write file

preconditions: cron_expr must be a valid 5-field cron expression
postconditions: Creates ~/Library/LaunchAgents directory if it doesn't exist
Writes plist file to ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.ascend.schedule.{schedule_name}.plist
Returns Path to written plist file

functions.name: load_plist
functions.description: Load a launchd plist using launchctl load command
postconditions: Returns True if launchctl load command succeeds
Returns False if plist doesn't exist or command fails or times out
side_effects: Executes subprocess 'launchctl load {plist_path}' with 10 second timeout
Catches all exceptions and returns False

functions.name: unload_plist
functions.description: Unload a launchd plist using launchctl unload command
postconditions: Returns True if launchctl unload command succeeds
Returns False if plist doesn't exist or command fails or times out
side_effects: Executes subprocess 'launchctl unload {plist_path}' with 10 second timeout
Catches all exceptions and returns False

functions.name: remove_plist
functions.description: Unload and delete a launchd plist file
postconditions: Calls unload_plist to unload the schedule
Deletes plist file if it exists
Returns True if file existed and was deleted
Returns False if file doesn't exist
side_effects: Calls unload_plist which executes launchctl unload

invariants: _DAY_MAP maps day names (long and short forms) to cron weekday numbers where 0=Sunday
_CRON_WEEKDAY_TO_PYTHON maps cron weekday numbers to Python weekday numbers where Python 0=Monday
_PLIST_TEMPLATE contains launchd plist XML template with placeholders for label, ascend_path, schedule_name, calendar_intervals, and log_path
Cron expressions use 5 fields: minute hour day-of-month month day-of-week
Launchd plist files are written to ~/Library/LaunchAgents/ with naming pattern com.ascend.schedule.{schedule_name}.plist
Biweekly schedules run on days 8-14 and 22-28 of each month (2nd and 4th occurrence)
Quarterly schedules run on day 1 of months 1, 4, 7, and 10
Cron OR semantics: when both day-of-month and day-of-week are restricted (not *), the schedule matches on EITHER condition
Next run computation searches up to 366 days ahead before giving up
All exceptions in load_plist, unload_plist are caught and converted to False return

data_access.rationale: Reads ascend executable path, reads/writes launchd plist files in ~/Library/LaunchAgents/, checks file existence

side_effects.rationale: Searches for ascend executable and checks plist file existence
Writes and deletes launchd plist configuration files
Executes launchctl commands to manage scheduled tasks

authority.rationale: This module owns the logic for converting schedule specifications to cron expressions, computing next run times, and managing macOS launchd plist files for the Ascend scheduler
